title: rivea wightiana
intro: Rivea wightiana, also known as the Himalayan moonseed or moonseed climber, is a perennial vine that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rivea wightiana include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve heart health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
